title: The Stolen Sandwich
case: At 12:30 PM, someone stole Margot's labeled tuna sandwich from the office kitchen fridge. Margot is furious and wants answers.

suspects.name: Raj
suspects.alibi: I was in the 12-to-1 team lunch in the conference room. I stepped out briefly around 12:30 to take a call from my kid's school — I paced in the hallway so I wouldn't disturb anyone.

suspects.name: Lena
suspects.alibi: I went to Sweetgreen for a kale salad. The line was long today, so I didn't get back until later than usual. I ate at my desk when I got in.

suspects.name: Ben
suspects.alibi: I had pad thai at my desk. I swung by the kitchen around 12:30 for cold brew — the pitcher was nearly empty so I topped it off from the jug like a good citizen.

suspects.name: Priya
suspects.alibi: I did my lunchtime walk with a podcast. I came through the kitchen around 12:40 to grab water, then went back to my desk.

suspects.name: Dex
suspects.alibi: I ducked into the kitchen around 12:20 to make tea. I remember Margot's tuna sandwich was on the top shelf — her label tape always cracks me up.

starter: The office kitchen has a motion-activated overhead light. It turned on at 12:28 PM and stayed on until 12:33 PM, then didn't trigger again until 12:50 PM.
Margot's sandwich was on the top shelf of the fridge, tucked behind the communal cold brew pitcher. You couldn't see or reach it without moving the pitcher aside.
The cold brew log — a clipboard the office keeps on the counter — shows the pitcher was topped off from the jug at 12:30 PM. The previous top-off was yesterday.

exonerations.raj: Raj's phone records show an incoming call from his kid's school at 12:31 that lasted eight minutes. Two coworkers saw him pacing the hallway outside the conference room during the entire call — nowhere near the kitchen.
exonerations.lena: Lena's Sweetgreen receipt is timestamped 12:48 PM and the shop is a 12-minute walk away. She didn't re-enter the office until 1:02, per the badge log.
exonerations.priya: Priya's podcast app shows continuous playback from 11:55 to 12:45, and her phone's GPS puts her three blocks from the office until 12:45. Her 12:50 kitchen entry matches the next motion-light trigger exactly.
exonerations.dex: Dex's keycard shows he was in the kitchen from 12:20 to 12:23 — and the cold brew pitcher was still visibly full at 12:25 on the fridge-cam frame. If he'd moved the pitcher aside to reach the sandwich, it would have been noticed.

reveal: No single clue names the thief — you had to chain them. Clue 1 narrows the theft to a five-minute window (12:28–12:33) when the kitchen was occupied. Clue 2 establishes that reaching the sandwich required moving the cold brew pitcher aside. Clue 3 logs exactly one cold brew top-off at 12:30 — the moment someone had both the pitcher in hand and the fridge open. Ben volunteered in his own alibi that he topped off the pitcher around 12:30. He put himself in the kitchen, in the window, with his hand on the one object concealing the sandwich. Raj was on the phone in the hallway, Dex left before the window, Priya arrived after it, and Lena wasn't even in the building.

title: The Missing Umbrella
case: At 4:15 PM on a rainy Tuesday, someone took Evelyn's distinctive red umbrella from the lobby umbrella stand at her apartment building. She needs it back before her dinner reservation.

suspects.name: Mrs. Chen
suspects.alibi: I watched my 4 PM show in my apartment. I did pop down to grab a package from the mailroom around 4 — but I went right back up.

suspects.name: Jake
suspects.alibi: I was recording an episode with my guest Diana from 3 to 5. She was on a video call from her place — she'll confirm we didn't pause.

suspects.name: Marcus
suspects.alibi: I took Pepper on our usual walk at 4. We cut it short because of the rain — back in about 15 minutes, both of us soaked through.

suspects.name: Sofia
suspects.alibi: I was studying at the coffee shop across the street. I walked back over around 4:15 in the rain — noticed the red umbrella in the stand but I already had my own.

suspects.name: Derek
suspects.alibi: I was fixing the radiator in 2B from 3:45 to 4:30. The tenant of 2B was home the whole time — made small talk while I worked.

starter: The lobby camera shows someone carrying Evelyn's red umbrella leaving through the front door at 4:16 PM. They arrive at the umbrella stand from the elevator — not from the street.
Maintenance mopped the lobby floor at 4:10 PM. No additional wet footprints appear on the floor between 4:10 and 4:16 — the thief was already dry when they reached the stand.
The elevator's usage log shows exactly one trip in the 4:10–4:20 window: called to the lobby from the 2nd floor at 4:14, returned to the 2nd floor at 4:17. No other elevator movement in that window.

exonerations.mrs-chen: Mrs. Chen's mailroom badge log puts her there from 3:52 to 3:58, not 4 PM, and a neighbor saw her ride the elevator back up to 3 at 3:59. She was in 3B at 4:14.
exonerations.jake: Jake's recording software logged an unbroken 3-to-5 PM session, and his guest Diana's video call records confirm continuous presence on camera. No elevator trip from 5 either.
exonerations.sofia: Sofia's coffee shop receipt is timestamped 4:23 PM; the lobby camera catches her walking in dripping wet at 4:28, well after the theft. She misremembered the time — she actually walked over later than she thought.
exonerations.derek: Derek's keycard shows continuous presence inside Apt 2B from 3:45 to 4:30, and the 2B tenant confirms he was there the entire time, radiator still disassembled at 4:14. The elevator trip from the 2nd floor wasn't him.

reveal: No single clue names Marcus — you had to triangulate. Clue 2 tells you the thief didn't walk in from the rainy street (no new wet footprints). Clue 1 confirms they came from the elevator. Clue 3 narrows it to one trip: someone riding from the 2nd floor down at 4:14 and back up at 4:17. Of the five suspects, only two had any reason to be on the 2nd floor — Marcus, who lives in 2C, and Derek, who was working in 2B. Derek's keycard and the 2B tenant both confirm he never left the apartment. That leaves Marcus — who claimed to be out walking Pepper in the rain, but was actually slipping down in the elevator to grab the umbrella and duck out unseen.

title: The Vanishing Cake
case: Someone ate the last slice of Nora's birthday cake from the break room fridge overnight. It was there at 6 PM when Nora left, and gone by 7 AM when she arrived.

suspects.name: Carlos
suspects.alibi: I was at the front desk all night. I did my rounds at 10 PM and 2 AM — on the 2 AM round I stopped by the break room to flick the lights off, but I didn't open the fridge.

suspects.name: Tamara
suspects.alibi: I cleaned the third floor from 8 to 10 PM. The break room is on the second floor — that's Jess's zone.

suspects.name: Owen
suspects.alibi: I stayed late debugging a server issue. Ordered pizza around 9 — ate it at my desk with the plastic fork from the delivery bag.

suspects.name: Jess
suspects.alibi: I cleaned the second floor from 8 to 10 PM. I saw the cake in the fridge on my rounds but I didn't touch it.

suspects.name: Maya
suspects.alibi: I left at 5:30 PM and didn't come back. I was at a concert downtown — I have the ticket stub.

starter: The break room door badge log shows three entries between 6 PM and 7 AM: at 8:12 PM, 8:47 PM, and 2:04 AM.

The break room fridge has an internal thermometer that logs temperature. A spike at 2:07 AM shows the door was open for roughly 90 seconds. No matching spikes occurred at 8:12 or 8:47 — whoever entered earlier didn't open the fridge.

The office dishwasher runs an automatic cycle every night at 11 PM and was empty and clean by 7 AM. A metal fork with chocolate frosting residue was left in the break room sink — placed after the 11 PM cycle completed.

exonerations.tamara: Tamara's 3rd-floor cleaning checklist and badge log confirm continuous 3rd-floor presence 8 to 10 PM, then she badged out at 10:12 PM and didn't return.
exonerations.owen: Owen badged out at 11:58 PM, before the fridge spike. His trash had the pizza delivery bag with a plastic fork — matching his alibi and not the metal fork left in the break room sink.
exonerations.jess: Jess's 2nd-floor rounds account for the 8:12 and 8:47 break room entries (lights, trash, countertops — no fridge). Her cleaning checklist requires all utensils go in the dishwasher, and she badged out at 10:15 PM, hours before the 2 AM fridge spike.
exonerations.maya: Maya's concert ticket was scanned at 7:45 PM downtown, a 40-minute drive from the office. She didn't badge back in to the building.

reveal: No clue named Carlos — you had to chain them. Clue 1 lists three break room entries, but Clue 2 narrows the actual theft to one: the 2:04 AM entry, the only one that opened the fridge. Clue 3 puts the fork in the sink after the 11 PM dishwasher cycle — past the evening cleaners' shift. Tamara and Jess had badged out by 10:15, Owen at 11:58, Maya was at a concert. Carlos's own alibi volunteers that he 'stopped by the break room' on his 2 AM round — the exact minute of the fridge spike. He gave himself away trying to sound helpful about his rounds.

title: The Library Book Bandit
case: A signed first edition of 'Dune' vanished from the locked display case at the Millbrook Public Library sometime between 2 PM and closing at 5 PM. The case was found unlocked.

suspects.name: Helen
suspects.alibi: I was running the children's reading hour from 2 to 3, then at the front desk until close. I have the only key to the display case.

suspects.name: Victor
suspects.alibi: I was browsing the sci-fi section from about 1:30 to 3. I left through the front door and Helen checked me out.

suspects.name: Dana
suspects.alibi: I was shelving returns in the nonfiction wing all afternoon. I never went near the display case area.

suspects.name: Keith
suspects.alibi: I was fixing a leaky pipe in the basement from 1 to 4 PM. You can check — the floor was still wet when I left.

suspects.name: Suki
suspects.alibi: I was studying at my usual table by the windows from noon to 4. I had my headphones on the whole time.

starter: The library's front door sensor logs one unexplained exit at 3:12 PM that Helen didn't log at the desk — she was buried in a returns pile at that moment and waved the person through without scanning.

The display case is in the alcove directly off the sci-fi aisle. Helen's key was missing from her desk drawer between 3 and 4 PM and was later found on the floor in the sci-fi aisle, on the path to the front door.

The library's guest Wi-Fi logs every device connection. One patron's phone stayed connected from 1:30 PM to 3:14 PM — two minutes past the claimed 3 PM exit — and disconnected the moment the 3:12 front-door exit fired.

exonerations.helen: Helen ran children's reading hour from 2 to 3, then stayed at the front desk visible to six patrons and the library assistant until close — she never entered the sci-fi alcove.
exonerations.dana: Dana's return-cart camera timestamped her continuously in the nonfiction wing from 2 to 4:30 PM — the opposite end of the library from the display case.
exonerations.keith: Keith's basement work order was logged by the plumbing company's dispatch at 12:58 and 4:02 PM bookending a wet-floor repair — basement keycard shows continuous presence, no upstairs trips.
exonerations.suki: Suki was visible at her window table to both Helen and the library assistant from noon to 4 PM. Her headphones and laptop never moved — the assistant checked on her twice.

reveal: Victor said Helen checked him out at 3 PM. But Clue 3 shows a patron's phone stayed on the library Wi-Fi until 3:14 PM — disconnecting at the exact moment of the 3:12 front-door exit Helen never logged (Clue 1). Of the suspects, only Victor claimed to have left at 3 — and his own story is 14 minutes short. Clue 2 closes the loop: the display-case key was dropped in the sci-fi aisle on the path from the alcove to the front door. Victor lifted the key while Helen was buried in returns, took the book from the alcove, and slipped out unscanned.

title: The Garden Gnome Caper
case: Mr. Patterson's prized garden gnome 'Gerald' disappeared from his front yard sometime between 10 PM last night and 6 AM this morning. Gerald has won 'Best Lawn Ornament' three years running.

suspects.name: Linda
suspects.alibi: I was watching TV until midnight and then went to bed. I didn't hear anything unusual.

suspects.name: Tom
suspects.alibi: I walk dogs in the neighborhood every evening. I finished my last walk at 9:30 and went straight home.

suspects.name: Wendy
suspects.alibi: I was at the HOA board meeting at the community center until 11 PM, then drove straight home. Three board members can confirm.

suspects.name: Brett
suspects.alibi: I was up late gaming online with my friends until about 2 AM. My game logs will show I was active the whole time.

suspects.name: Diane
suspects.alibi: I was asleep by 9 PM. I take melatonin every night and I'm a very heavy sleeper. My husband can confirm.

starter: Mr. Patterson's Ring camera was offline from 11:45 PM to 12:10 AM due to a Wi-Fi outage. Mr. Patterson lives in a duplex and shares the router with the tenant of the other unit — the outage affected only their two addresses.
The stolen gnome weighs 47 pounds. Fresh wheelbarrow tracks begin in the soft soil next to Mr. Patterson's lawn and end at the gate that divides his half of the duplex yard from the other half.

Router outages on that ISP trigger a push notification to every device on the network's admin account. The notification log shows the 11:45 PM outage alert was opened and dismissed on one phone within 30 seconds of firing — meaning the account holder was awake.

exonerations.tom: Tom's last dog-walking client confirmed dropoff at 9:25 PM three blocks away, and his car's GPS shows it never left his driveway after 9:40 PM. His house is four blocks from Mr. Patterson's — no route in or out on foot in that window.
exonerations.wendy: Wendy's HOA meeting attendance and the community center's alarm log confirm she was there until 11:05 PM, and her car's GPS shows her home by 11:20 — after which she stayed put until morning.
exonerations.brett: Brett's gaming session logs confirm continuous input activity from 10:30 PM to 2:15 AM with no breaks longer than 2 minutes — not enough time to cross the street and back with a wheelbarrow.
exonerations.diane: Diane's smart watch sleep log shows uninterrupted deep-sleep heart rate from 8:55 PM to 6:40 AM, and her home's exterior cam shows no one left the house all night.

reveal: No clue named Linda outright. Clue 1 established that only two households share the affected router — Mr. Patterson and whoever rents the other half of the duplex. Clue 3 is the tell: the shared router's outage alert was dismissed within 30 seconds by the account holder — meaning that person was awake when the camera went dark, despite Linda's claim she was asleep by midnight. Clue 2 shows wheelbarrow tracks ending at the gate between the two halves of the duplex — a direct-access path only the other tenant has. Linda was informed, awake, and had the only route.

title: The Coffee Shop Tip Jar
case: The tip jar at Brewed Awakening disappeared during the morning rush between 8 and 9 AM. It had roughly $85 in cash and was sitting on the counter by the register.

suspects.name: Nina
suspects.alibi: I was behind the espresso machine the whole rush. I didn't step out from behind the counter once — we were slammed.

suspects.name: Ravi
suspects.alibi: I came in at 8:15, ordered my usual cortado, and sat in the corner booth reading until 9. I pay with my phone.

suspects.name: Greta
suspects.alibi: I picked up a batch of online orders at 8:30. I was in and out in two minutes — I had six more stops.

suspects.name: Frank
suspects.alibi: I clocked in at 8:45 and went straight to stocking the pastry case. The tip jar was already gone when I noticed at 9.

suspects.name: Zoe
suspects.alibi: I was shadowing Nina and learning the register from 7:30 to 9. I was right next to the counter the entire time.

starter: The security camera covering the register was bumped off its angle at 8:18 AM — up until that minute it was pointing at the counter; after, it pointed at the ceiling. The last clear frame shows the tip jar on the counter next to the register.

The espresso machine has a per-drink log: 34 shots pulled between 8:00 and 9:00, gap never longer than 45 seconds. The machine sits at the far end of the counter, six feet from the register — whoever was pulling shots never moved.

At 8:17 AM a customer paid cash and received change — the POS system logged a $4.75 transaction and a drawer open. The person who operated the register in that minute was the last staff member confirmed at the counter before the camera was bumped.

exonerations.nina: Nina's the one pulling the 34 espresso shots logged by the machine — she was pinned to the espresso station at the far end of the counter, six feet from the register, all rush. Receipts confirm she never rang up the drawer.
exonerations.ravi: Ravi's corner booth is eight feet from the counter with a bench in between. Three other regulars confirm he was reading there from 8:15 to 9, and his 8:16 cortado was rung up from behind the counter — he was on the customer side of it.
exonerations.greta: Greta's delivery app GPS log shows she arrived at 8:32 AM and left at 8:34 AM — well after the camera was bumped at 8:18. She never approached the counter.
exonerations.frank: Frank's timecard confirms he badged in through the back entrance at 8:47 AM, nearly half an hour after the camera was tampered with.

reveal: No clue named anyone directly. Clue 2 pins the espresso-shot puller to the far end of the counter the whole rush — Nina, per her alibi. But the 8:17 POS cash transaction (Clue 3) required someone to operate the register, six feet away — not Nina. That leaves a second staff member at the counter. Clue 1 times the camera tampering one minute later at 8:18. Zoe's alibi said she was 'right next to Nina' — but Nina wasn't at the register. Zoe was operating it alone, rang up the 8:17 cash customer, bumped the camera the next minute, and pocketed the jar before Frank clocked in.

title: The Parking Lot Scratch
case: Someone keyed a long scratch down the side of Dr. Patel's new Tesla in the hospital parking garage between 7 AM and 3 PM. She's checked the garage cameras but the angle doesn't cover her spot.

suspects.name: Nurse Kim
suspects.alibi: I was on shift in the ER from 6 AM to 2 PM. I parked on level 2, same as always. I didn't go back to my car until my shift ended.

suspects.name: Gary
suspects.alibi: I was at the booth at the garage entrance all day. I see every car come in and out but I don't walk the levels.

suspects.name: Dr. Mason
suspects.alibi: I was in surgery from 8 AM to noon, then in my office doing paperwork until 4. I parked on level 3.

suspects.name: Phil
suspects.alibi: I arrived at 9 AM to visit my wife and left at 1 PM. I parked on level 1 near the elevator.

suspects.name: Angela
suspects.alibi: I was in back-to-back meetings from 8 to 3. I parked in the reserved spots on level 2, right next to Dr. Patel.

starter: The reserved parking section on level 2 has three spots. One belongs to Dr. Patel. The other two are assigned to an administrator and to the Chief of Medicine — the latter has been on vacation in Greece for ten days.
The scratch runs down the driver's side of Dr. Patel's Tesla — the side facing the immediately adjacent reserved spot.

Tesla Sentry Mode recorded a blurry figure at 7:43 AM leaning against the driver's side with a large cardboard box pressed against the door for nearly a minute. The box had a distinctive red logo. An Instagram post from the hospital's admin wing the same morning, tagged #newoffice, featured the same red-logo box captioned 'big delivery to the office today!'

exonerations.nurse-kim: Nurse Kim's badge shows continuous presence in the ER from 5:58 AM to 2:04 PM. She parks on level 2 but in general staff parking, not the reserved section — a separate row.
exonerations.gary: Gary's booth has a camera and a log — he was taking tickets continuously until 3 PM. He works out of the entrance booth, not the reserved section.
exonerations.dr-mason: Dr. Mason parks on level 3 (not level 2 reserved), and his surgical team confirmed he was scrubbed in from 7:55 AM to 12:20 PM. His office keycard shows continuous occupancy until 4 PM.
exonerations.phil: Phil's visitor badge was issued at 9:03 AM and returned at 12:58 PM — well after the 7:43 AM incident. He parked on level 1 near the elevator, nowhere near the reserved section.

reveal: No clue named Angela. Clue 1 narrows the reserved-spot residents to two people besides Dr. Patel: an administrator and the Chief — and the Chief is in Greece. Clue 2 places the scratch source in the immediately adjacent spot, meaning the administrator's. Clue 3 ties a distinctive red-logo box to an Instagram post from the admin wing that same morning. Among the five suspects, only Angela is an administrator. Tesla Sentry caught her pressing her newly delivered box against Dr. Patel's door — and she posted a photo of the very same box online, not realizing it would identify her.

title: The Missing Mic
case: The open mic night wireless microphone vanished from The Blue Note bar between soundcheck at 5 PM and doors at 7 PM. It's a $400 Shure and the bar owner wants it back.

suspects.name: Charlie
suspects.alibi: I set up the mic at soundcheck, then went to grab dinner at the taco truck. I got back at 6:45 and noticed it was gone.

suspects.name: Mia
suspects.alibi: I was prepping the bar from 4 to 7. I was behind the bar the whole time — the stage is across the room from me.

suspects.name: Devon
suspects.alibi: I came in at 6 to warm up backstage. I brought my own mic because I always do — I don't trust house equipment.

suspects.name: Sam
suspects.alibi: I was doing homework in the back office from 4 to 7. I only came out to grab a soda from behind the bar at 5:30.

suspects.name: Liz
suspects.alibi: I showed up at 6:30 to sign up for a slot. I waited by the sign-up sheet near the door until it opened at 7.

starter: The bar's back door was propped open from 5:30 to 6:30 PM to air out the room. It leads to an alley. Exactly one vehicle parked in the alley during that window — a car with a touring-musicians' union sticker on the rear window, arriving at 6:00 PM.

The missing mic is a specific Shure SM58 that the bar registered in Shure's rewards program. Six weeks ago the bar received an email from a performer offering to buy this exact unit: 'that mic sounds incredible on my voice — I'd love to own this one eventually.' The bar declined.

Every performer who's done a set at The Blue Note in the past year has a rider on file — a standard list of stage preferences. One suspect's rider, from a booking last fall, specifies 'Shure SM58 house mic preferred; I travel without my own.' Tonight the same person told staff the opposite.

exonerations.charlie: Charlie's taco truck receipt is timestamped 5:15 PM, and the truck is a 20-minute walk away. He arrived back at 6:44 per the alley cam — after the back door was already closed.
exonerations.mia: Mia's bar POS system logged 43 drink orders rung up between 5:30 and 7 PM — she was behind the bar with a line of customers the entire time. She never went backstage.
exonerations.sam: Sam's soda run at 5:30 was confirmed by Mia, who poured it. Sam went straight back to the office and the office door security cam shows no exits until 7.
exonerations.liz: Liz signed in on the sheet at 6:33 PM and chatted with the doorman for 27 minutes straight — the doorman confirms she never moved. She arrived on foot; no car in the alley.

reveal: Clue 3 catches one suspect in a lie against their own on-file rider — it says 'travel without my own mic, prefer house Shure,' but tonight that person claimed the opposite. Clue 2 shows a performer specifically offered to buy this exact mic six weeks ago. Clue 1 places a car with a touring-musicians' union sticker in the alley during the open-back-door window — the only vehicle there. Of the five suspects, only Devon is a touring performer. He arrived claiming he'd brought his own mic, walked the house Shure out the open back door to his car, and contradicted his own rider doing it.

title: The Test Answer Leak
case: Someone photographed tomorrow's final exam and posted it to a group chat. Professor Walsh discovered the leak at 4 PM when a student reported it. The exam was only printed at 2 PM.

suspects.name: Jordan
suspects.alibi: I picked up the printed exams from the copy room at 2:15 and delivered them straight to Professor Walsh's locked office. I didn't look at them.

suspects.name: Elena
suspects.alibi: I printed the exams at 2 PM and put them in the sealed envelope Professor Walsh prepared. I handed the envelope to Jordan.

suspects.name: Marcus
suspects.alibi: I was in the library studying with my group from 1 to 5. We had a study room reserved — four people can vouch for me.

suspects.name: Prof. Lin
suspects.alibi: I was in my office next to Walsh's all afternoon. I didn't see anyone go in or out after Jordan's delivery.

suspects.name: Tanya
suspects.alibi: I was at the front desk of the department office from 1 to 5. The only person who asked for Walsh's office key was Jordan at 2:20.

starter: The leaked photo's EXIF metadata shows it was taken at 2:22 PM. An expert matched the overhead-light flicker frequency in the photo to Walsh's office lighting — not the copy room, not a classroom, not the department office. The photo was taken inside Walsh's office.

Walsh was in a faculty meeting from 1 to 4 PM, verified by sign-in and four colleagues — she never returned to her office during that window. Her office has keycard access and logs every entry; only one entry occurred between 1 and 4 PM.
The sealed envelope was found on Walsh's desk with the seal cut open, and a small pair of scissors with faint envelope-paper fibers was later found on a TA workstation in the shared TA room.

exonerations.elena: Elena's copy room badge log shows she didn't leave the copy room between 1 and 3 PM, and she handed the sealed envelope off without opening it (the department's signed receipt confirms this). She had no keycard access to Walsh's office.
exonerations.marcus-t: Marcus is a student — no keycard access to Walsh's office. His library study room booking and keycard log also confirm continuous presence from 12:55 PM to 5:10 PM with four witnesses.
exonerations.professor-lin: Prof. Lin's own office is keycard-locked and her log shows continuous presence from 12:45 PM to 4:15 PM. Her door faces Walsh's — she'd have seen anyone approach — and she has no key to Walsh's office.
exonerations.tanya: Tanya's front-desk sign-out log tracks every key handout. She never left her post, and she records every request — the log shows only one Walsh-office key sign-out that afternoon, by the TA at 2:20 PM.

reveal: No clue named Jordan. Clue 1 places the photo inside Walsh's office at 2:22 PM via light-frequency matching. Clue 2 rules out Walsh herself and establishes only one keycard entry occurred in the window. Tanya's log (see her exoneration) shows that one entry belonged to 'the TA' — and Jordan is the only TA in the suspect list. Clue 3 closes the loop: the envelope was cut open with scissors left on a TA workstation. Jordan had sole access, sole motive to route the key through the front desk, and physical evidence tying the opening to his own workspace.

title: The Gym Bag Switcheroo
case: Someone swapped Pete's gym bag (containing his wallet, watch, and AirPods) with an identical empty bag in the locker room between 6 and 7 AM during his workout.

suspects.name: Coach Ray
suspects.alibi: I was on the gym floor training a client from 5:45 to 7. I never went into the men's locker room during that time.

suspects.name: Dennis
suspects.alibi: I was doing my usual cardio routine on the treadmill from 6 to 6:45. I showered and left by 7:10.

suspects.name: Lisa
suspects.alibi: I was at the front desk the whole morning. I don't go into the locker rooms — that's not my job.

suspects.name: Wayne
suspects.alibi: I did weights from 6:15 to 7. I have an assigned locker next to Pete's — I use the same one every day. I didn't see anything suspicious.

suspects.name: Hank
suspects.alibi: I was cleaning the pool area from 6 to 7:30. The pool is on the other side of the building from the locker rooms.

starter: The pro shop sold one identical bag to Pete's at 5:55 AM this morning, paid in cash. This bag model is discontinued — the sale emptied that SKU from the shelf. The pro shop is a 30-second walk from the men's locker room.

The men's locker room door opens with a fob that logs every entry. Between 5:45 and 7 AM, one fob registered at 5:52 AM and didn't register an exit until 7:05 AM — a 73-minute stretch inside the locker room. No other fob that morning showed a similar pattern.

The gym's overhead CCTV caught Pete placing his bag in Locker 14 at 5:57 AM. Locker 15 — the unit immediately adjacent — is permanently assigned to a daily regular per the gym's locker-assignment roster.

exonerations.coach-ray: Coach Ray's client confirmed a full uninterrupted session from 5:45 to 7 AM on the gym floor, visible on the gym's main-floor camera the whole time. He never badged into the men's locker room.
exonerations.dennis: Dennis's treadmill data log shows continuous activity from 6:02 to 6:44 AM with no pauses longer than 30 seconds. His locker room entry fob fired at 6:46 AM and exited at 7:10 AM — well after the swap.
exonerations.lisa: Lisa processed 47 check-ins and a pro shop sale from the front desk between 5:30 and 7:30 AM — she never left her post. Her fob has no entries for the men's locker room.
exonerations.hank: Hank's keycard log shows him in the pool area from 5:58 to 7:32 AM, and the pool deck cam has him in frame continuously. He was nowhere near the men's locker room.

reveal: No clue named Wayne. Clue 3 ties Wayne to the adjacent locker (Locker 15, his assigned daily locker, right next to Pete's Locker 14). Clue 2 shows one fob stayed inside the locker room for 73 straight minutes — from 5:52 AM to 7:05 AM, impossible to square with Wayne's claim that his weight session ran 6:15 to 7. Clue 1 times the decoy purchase at 5:55, three minutes after that 5:52 fob entry. Wayne arrived 25 minutes earlier than he claimed, bought the decoy bag in cash, waited in the locker room for Pete to head to the floor, swapped the bags, and emerged at 7:05 pretending his 'workout' had just ended.

title: The Potluck Saboteur
case: Someone dumped an entire shaker of salt into Rita's award-winning chili at the neighborhood potluck. The chili was set out at 5 PM and declared inedible by 5:30 PM.

suspects.name: Barb
suspects.alibi: I was at the dessert table the whole time arranging my seven-layer bars. I was at the opposite end of the food line from the chili.

suspects.name: Greg
suspects.alibi: I was running around setting up tables and chairs. I didn't go near the food once — I hadn't even grabbed a plate yet.

suspects.name: Yolanda
suspects.alibi: I was helping Rita carry dishes from her car. I set down the cornbread right next to the chili and then went to park.

suspects.name: Neil
suspects.alibi: I was watching the kids on the bounce house from 4:45 to 5:30. My wife waved at me the whole time.

suspects.name: Dot
suspects.alibi: I was the first person to taste the chili at 5:15. It was already salty by then! I immediately told Rita something was wrong.

starter: Three neighbors served themselves chili between 5:00 and 5:10 PM and all said it tasted perfect. The first complaint came at 5:15 PM.
The large salt shaker kept next to the chili was found nearly empty; an identical shaker at the opposite end of the table was untouched. The sabotage happened at the chili end.

A neighbor's photo taken at 5:12 PM shows the food table with one person standing alone at the chili pot, salt shaker in hand. The photo is angled from behind — the face isn't visible — but a small gold HOA board pin is clearly on the shirt. Those pins are issued only to current HOA board members.

exonerations.rita-neighbor: Barb's seven-layer bars were being photographed by two neighbors at the dessert table from 5:05 to 5:20 — she was in every photo, posing with her tray, 40 feet from the chili.
exonerations.greg: Greg was captured on a neighbor's doorbell camera carrying folding chairs at 5:12 PM at the far end of the yard. Greg runs the event as a volunteer but isn't on the HOA board — no pin.
exonerations.yolanda: Yolanda's car was parked three blocks away. Neighbors confirm she didn't return from parking until 5:20 PM — after the chili was already salted. She's also not on the HOA board.
exonerations.neil: Neil was at the bounce house from 4:45 to 5:30 continuously; his wife and four other parents confirm they saw him there without interruption. He's not on the HOA board either.

reveal: Clue 1 narrows the sabotage window to 5:10–5:15 (chili fine through 5:10, first complaint at 5:15). Clue 2 locates the tampering at the chili end of the table. Clue 3 shows one person alone at the chili pot at 5:12 holding the salt shaker — face not visible, but wearing a gold HOA board pin. Of the five suspects, only Dot is on the HOA board; she's the Treasurer. She dumped the salt at 5:12, then 'discovered' the ruined chili at 5:15 to cast herself as the concerned taster instead of the saboteur.

title: The Dog Park Dognapping
case: Someone walked off with Olivia's French Bulldog, Biscuit, from the Riverside Dog Park at approximately 3:30 PM while Olivia was refilling water bowls at the far end of the park.

suspects.name: Paul
suspects.alibi: I had three dogs with me from 3 to 4 PM. I was in the large dog section the whole time. Biscuit is a small dog — she'd be in the other section.

suspects.name: Trish
suspects.alibi: I was chatting with Maria by the benches near the entrance from 3:15 to 3:45. My poodle Coco was playing off-leash.

suspects.name: Evan
suspects.alibi: I was jogging the loop trail around the park. I passed through but I don't stop — I was doing intervals and had my headphones in.

suspects.name: Maria
suspects.alibi: I was talking to Trish on the bench by the entrance from 3:15 to 3:45. My Lab, Bruno, was right at my feet.

suspects.name: Noah
suspects.alibi: I just moved to the neighborhood. I was at the park from 3 to 4 scoping it out for my puppy. I don't even have a leash with me.

starter: A park bench camera captured someone walking out the side gate at 3:32 PM carrying a small French Bulldog in a blue puffer jacket — Biscuit's signature outfit.
The side gate is accessible only from inside the small dog section — it's double-gated off from the large dog section. A small-dog collar-style leash was found dropped at the gate after the theft.

At 3:30 PM the small dog section contained three adults: Olivia refilling water bowls at the far end, one adult with two shih tzus visible in frame on the main lawn the whole five minutes around 3:30, and one adult standing alone near the side gate without a visible dog.

exonerations.paul: Paul's three client dogs are all over 50 pounds and confined to the large dog section by the double-gate rule. Park cam shows all three leashed at his side continuously from 3 to 4 — he never crossed into the small dog section.
exonerations.trish: Trish was visible to the entrance bench camera from 3:15 to 3:45, with her poodle Coco in frame the entire time — on the opposite side of the park from the small dog section.
exonerations.evan: Evan's fitness tracker GPS shows he was on the outer loop trail at 3:30 PM, outside the fenced dog park entirely. He never entered either section.
exonerations.maria: Maria was sitting next to Trish on the entrance bench from 3:15 to 3:45, confirmed on the bench cam, with her Lab Bruno lying at her feet — not in the small dog section.

reveal: No clue named Noah. Clue 1 shows someone leaving with Biscuit in her distinctive jacket at 3:32. Clue 2 narrows the culprit to someone inside the small dog section — and places a dropped small-dog leash at the gate. Clue 3 identifies three adults in the section: Olivia herself, one adult with two shih tzus (continuously on-camera), and one adult standing alone near the gate without a dog. The first two don't fit; the third does. Of the five suspects, only Noah was in the small dog section without a dog of his own — and he specifically claimed to have 'no leash,' which is the exact thing dropped at the gate.

title: The Dorm Room DJ
case: Someone blasted music from Room 412 at 3 AM, waking up the entire fourth floor. The room belongs to Casey, who insists they were asleep and their speaker was hijacked via Bluetooth.

suspects.name: Casey
suspects.alibi: I was dead asleep. My speaker was still on from studying earlier. Someone must have connected to it — it doesn't have a password.

suspects.name: Riley
suspects.alibi: I was up late working on a paper. I heard the music start and immediately went to Casey's door to complain, but nobody answered.

suspects.name: Alex
suspects.alibi: I was FaceTiming my partner until 2:30 AM, then went to sleep. The music woke me up at 3.

suspects.name: Jamie
suspects.alibi: I got back from a late study session at the library around 2:45. I was brushing my teeth when the music started.

suspects.name: Morgan
suspects.alibi: I was asleep in my room. I'm a heavy sleeper — I didn't hear anything until Riley came banging on my door at 3:10.

starter: Casey's Bluetooth speaker has a range of about 30 feet. Only rooms 410, 411, 413, and 414 are within range of Room 412. Any other room is too far to connect.

The 'Revenge Mix' playlist was created two days ago under a Spotify account paying through the university's subsidized student-music plan. Residential Life's records show only one Floor 4 resident enrolled in that subsidy — the resident of Room 410.
University Residence Life records show three formal noise complaints filed against Casey in the past month. All three were filed by the same resident — the occupant of Room 410.

exonerations.casey: Casey's phone sleep tracker recorded continuous deep sleep from 1:15 to 3:02 AM, and their door camera shows them in bed asleep when the music started — they physically couldn't have triggered it.
exonerations.alex-k: Alex's room (415) is out of Bluetooth range from Room 412 — too far down the hall to connect to Casey's speaker.
exonerations.jamie: Jamie's room (414) is in range, but their library keycard shows they returned at 2:47 AM and their door cam shows they didn't leave their room after. Their Spotify account was also inactive that night.
exonerations.morgan: Morgan's room (401) is on the opposite end of the floor, out of Bluetooth range. Their RA duty log also shows no activity between 11 PM and 3:10 AM.

reveal: No clue named Riley by name. Clue 1 limited the culprit to rooms 410, 411, 413, or 414 by Bluetooth range. Clue 2 traced the playlist to the Room 410 resident via the university music subsidy. Clue 3 showed the Room 410 resident had filed three noise complaints against Casey in the past month — motive. Three independent facts triangulated on one door. Of the suspects, only Riley lives in 410. Riley connected to Casey's unsecured speaker from next door, blasted the playlist, then played the outraged neighbor to frame Casey.

title: The Wedding Gift Grab
case: An envelope containing $500 cash disappeared from the wedding gift table at the Riverside Inn reception. The bride noticed it missing during the card opening at 10 PM. It was placed on the table at 6 PM.

suspects.name: Uncle Rob
suspects.alibi: I was at the bar most of the night. I put my card on the table at 6 when I arrived and then hit the dance floor around 8.

suspects.name: Claire
suspects.alibi: I was coordinating everything all night. I checked the gift table at 7 PM to make sure it looked nice, but I didn't touch any envelopes.

suspects.name: Dante
suspects.alibi: I was busing tables and restocking the bar in my dark blue venue uniform — hands full of dishes most of the night. I walked past the gift table a few times but didn't stop.

suspects.name: Megan
suspects.alibi: I was with the bridal party doing photos from 5 to 7, then at the head table for dinner, then dancing. I barely left the main room.

suspects.name: Tyler
suspects.alibi: I arrived late around 8:30 in my dark blue suit — my flight was delayed. Dropped my card on the table, went to find the groom.

starter: The venue's QR-invite scanner logs every guest on arrival. Of the five people on the suspect list, one scanned in at 7:15 PM — despite claiming in conversation they hadn't arrived until after 8.

The $500 envelope from the groom's boss was distinctive: thick, with a gold-foil company logo on the back. A hallway security camera captured someone in a dark blue suit pocketing a gold-foil envelope from the gift table at 7:24 PM and walking toward the restrooms. The camera didn't catch the face.

Three men on the suspect list planned to wear dark blue suits that night: Uncle Rob, Dante (venue uniform), and Tyler. Bar-tab receipts, kitchen camera footage, and the hallway camera at 7:24 establish that only one of the three was unaccounted for at that moment.

exonerations.uncle-rob: Uncle Rob's bar tab shows continuous drink orders from 6:15 PM to 9:45 PM. The bartender remembers him fondly — he was at the bar, in frame on the bar cam, throughout the 7:24 window.
exonerations.claire: Claire's coordinator assistant was with her during the 7 PM gift table check, and the gift table is on the hallway camera — she didn't touch any envelope. She wore a red dress.
exonerations.dante: Dante's manager confirmed he was on dish duty in the kitchen from 7:00 to 8:45 PM in his dark blue venue uniform. The kitchen camera shows continuous presence — he never stepped into the hallway at 7:24.
exonerations.megan: Megan is in every bridal party photo from 5 to 7 in a bridesmaid dress, then seated at the head table — which faces the gift table — from 7 to 9:30 PM, visible to 120 guests.

reveal: No clue named Tyler. Clue 1 caught one suspect lying about arrival time — checked in at 7:15 despite claiming post-8. Clue 2 placed the theft at 7:24, committed by someone in a dark blue suit. Clue 3 identified three dark-blue-suit wearers and accounted for two: Uncle Rob (on bar cam continuously) and Dante (in kitchen on dish duty). Only one dark-blue-suit wearer was both lying about arrival time and unaccounted for at 7:24 — Tyler. He came early, pocketed the biggest-looking envelope, and later acted as if he'd just walked in.
